summary: This skill helps create publication-ready visualizations of neural population activity data from CSV files, generating heatmaps and population peristimulus time histograms (PSTHs) with proper statistical representations.
preconditions: Validate CSV file exists and is readable before processing
Check for required columns (trial, time) and at least one neuron column
Handle missing values with appropriate interpolation or exclusion
strategy: Load and validate CSV data structure (trial, time, neuron_* columns)
Reshape data into trials × time × neurons array for efficient computation
Calculate trial-averaged firing rates and population statistics (mean, SEM)
AVOID: Missing neuron columns: Always filter columns with `neuron_` prefix and handle cases where no neuron columns exist
AVOID: Irregular time sampling: Use `pivot_table` with aggregation to handle duplicate time points per trial
AVOID: Memory issues with large datasets: Process data in chunks or use memory-efficient pandas operations instead of loading entire arrays
